commit.message:
fix(vault): expire secret refs at resolution time

Summary:
- prune expired secret refs when resolving opaque tokens, not just when issuing new ones
- add a regression test proving expired refs stop resolving after the TTL window

Rationale:
- sessions that stop issuing refs still need the one-hour secret-ref lifetime to be enforced consistently

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/vault/refs.py backend/tests/test_secret_ref_tools.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_secret_ref_tools.py tests/test_vault_tools.py tests/test_tools_api.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_specialists.py tests/test_delegation.py
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(warnings): remove test and CI warnings

Summary:

  fake transport fully satisfies `EtherCAT.Bus.Transport`
- bump CI from OTP 28.0 to 28.1 and refresh the cache key to avoid the

- align CI with the OTP version required to avoid the known regex

commit.message:
feat(vault): add session-scoped secret references

Summary:
- add opaque session-scoped secret references and resolve them at tool execution time
- expose a new `get_secret_ref` path so skills can use stored credentials without pulling raw values back into model context
- update the Season 1 trust-boundary docs and Moltbook skill guidance to reflect the safer secret flow

Rationale:
- raw `get_secret()` retrieval kept a trust-boundary gap open because the secret value had to pass back through the agent loop
- session-bound refs narrow that gap while keeping authenticated downstream tool usage practical

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/vault/refs.py backend/src/tools/secret_ref_tools.py backend/src/agent/factory.py backend/src/agent/specialists.py backend/src/plugins/registry.py backend/tests/test_secret_ref_tools.py backend/tests/test_specialists.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_secret_ref_tools.py tests/test_vault_tools.py tests/test_tools_api.py tests/test_tool_registry.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_specialists.py tests/test_delegation.py
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(issue-4): use LLM-aware estimator for circuit breaker cost resolution (with E2E) (#23)

* fix(issue-4): use LLM-aware estimator for circuit breaker cost resolution

* test(e2e): add regression test for LLM cost resolution

* test(issue-4): add UC-09/UC-16/UC-17 integration coverage and fix E2E test

- Remove fake test_header_hint.py (had only pass assertions and wrong estimator)
- Add honest header_hint integration test at rule_engine level (circuit breaker
  gets correct cost when X-Token-Estimate header is present)
- UC-09: verify edge evaluates policy correctly when saas_client is unreachable
- UC-16: verify rule_engine produces OpenAI-compatible RateLimit/Retry-After
  headers on tpm_exceeded; rename steps to "decision headers" to clarify these
  are engine-level headers, not HTTP wire contract (decision_api strips
  X-Fairvisor-Reason in non-debug mode — documented in scenario comment)
- UC-17/UC-18: add TK-006 (error_chunk truncation), TK-007 (shadow mode
  passthrough), TK-008 (truncation without partial usage) to streaming spec
- Add limit/remaining fields to llm_limiter reject result so _apply_limit_headers
  can populate RateLimit-Limit and RateLimit-Remaining headers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): make UC-09 actually exercise saas_client failure path

Previously the UC-09 scenario used an allow decision, which exits before
calling queue_event — so saas_unreachable flag had no effect on the test.

Now uses a reject decision (TPM exceeded) which does call queue_event.
Track saas_event_attempts in mock to assert the call was made.
New assertion: queue_event was attempted AND the decision is still valid,
proving SaaS audit failure does not block enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(e2e): P0 e2e tests for Issue #4 — OpenAI contract and streaming truncation

- decision_api.lua: wire llm_limiter.build_error_response in reject path;
  tpm_exceeded/tpd_exceeded/prompt_tokens_exceeded/max_tokens_per_request_exceeded
  now return Content-Type: application/json with OpenAI error structure before ngx.exit(429)
- tests/e2e/test_llm_openai_contract.py: 5 tests verifying 429 body is valid OpenAI
  JSON (error.type=rate_limit_error, error.code=rate_limit_exceeded, message references reason)
  plus Content-Type and RateLimit-* headers still present
- tests/e2e/test_llm_streaming.py: 5 tests verifying mid-stream truncation at
  max_completion_tokens=10 — events 1+2 pass, event 3 triggers truncation,
  response ends with finish_reason=length and [DONE]
- tests/e2e/policy_llm_openai_contract.json: TPM=500, header_hint estimator
- tests/e2e/policy_llm_streaming.json: TPM=100k, defaults.max_completion_tokens=10,
  streaming.buffer_tokens=1, graceful_close mode
- tests/e2e/sse_fixture.txt: 3 SSE events (4+5+6 tokens) + [DONE]
- tests/e2e/mock-streaming-backend.conf: nginx serving sse_fixture.txt as SSE
- docker-compose.test.yml: edge_llm_openai_contract (18089), mock_streaming_backend,
  edge_llm_streaming (18090)
- conftest.py: fixtures for edge_llm_openai_contract_base_url and edge_llm_streaming_base_url

All 497 unit+integration tests continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve code-quality warnings and add new e2e tests to smoke CI

- test_llm_openai_contract.py: remove unused 'import json'
- test_llm_streaming.py: remove unused 'import pytest'
- conftest.py: invert fixture conditionals (if not ready: skip / return url)
  to eliminate mixed implicit/explicit return warnings from static analysis
- bin/ci/run_e2e_smoke.sh: add test_llm_openai_contract.py and
  test_llm_streaming.py to smoke test suite so new E2E tests are
  gated in CI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): replace nginx streaming mock with Python server to fix 405 on POST

nginx's static file module only handles GET/HEAD — serving sse_fixture.txt
via try_files returned 405 for POST /v1/chat/completions, causing all 5
streaming e2e tests to fail.

Replace mock_streaming_backend with a minimal Python http.server that:
- Accepts POST on any path → returns SSE fixture with correct Content-Type
- Accepts GET / → returns "ok" (used by healthcheck)
- Embeds the SSE content inline (4+5+6 token events for truncation test)

Remove broken mock-streaming-backend.conf and sse_fixture.txt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(streaming): preserve buffered events when truncation fires mid-batch

When nginx buffers multiple SSE events into a single body_filter call,
the early return on truncation was discarding events that had already
passed the token budget check and been added to `out`.

Fix: return table_concat(out) .. _build_termination(ctx) so that events
processed before the truncation threshold are forwarded to the client.

For single-event calls (the common streaming case), out is empty so
table_concat({}) = "" — identical behavior to before.

This was caught by the e2e streaming truncation test where the Python
mock sends the complete SSE fixture as a single HTTP response body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(policy): decouple MCP access from native tool mode

Summary:
- make MCP tool availability depend on MCP policy mode rather than native tool mode
- add a regression test covering balanced native tools with MCP ask-first enabled

Rationale:
- the MCP toggle should work independently so users can keep native tools constrained while still allowing approved external calls

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/tools/policy.py backend/tests/test_tools_api.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_tools_api.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_specialists.py tests/test_delegation.py
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(policy): add explicit MCP access modes

Summary:
- add a persisted MCP access policy with disabled, approval, and full modes
- force approval for MCP calls in ask-first mode and expose the setting in the UI
- update Season 1 roadmap docs to reflect the new trust-boundary progress

Rationale:
- external MCP tools needed a clearer control surface than the old all-or-nothing behavior
- this gives Seraph a more honest trust model for connected runtimes without widening scope

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/tools/policy.py backend/src/tools/approval.py backend/src/agent/factory.py backend/src/agent/specialists.py backend/src/observer/context.py backend/src/observer/manager.py backend/src/db/models.py backend/src/db/engine.py backend/src/app.py backend/src/api/settings.py backend/src/api/tools.py backend/tests/test_settings_mcp_policy_mode.py backend/tests/test_tools_api.py backend/tests/test_approval_tools.py

- cd frontend && npm test
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(context): avoid caching failed tokenizer loads

Summary:
- cache only successful tokenizer initialization results
- keep the approximate fallback path for offline failures without memoizing them forever
- add a regression test for transient tokenizer recovery

Rationale:
- address PR feedback about a transient tokenizer failure poisoning token counting for the life of the process
- preserve degraded-mode availability without sacrificing eventual recovery when the encoding becomes available

Tests:
- cd backend && python3 -m py_compile src/agent/context_window.py tests/test_context_window.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_context_window.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): correct map auth and overlay behavior

Treat the SPA entry routes as public so direct requests to /map,
/decoders, /settings, and /about return the app shell and let
the frontend show the login screen instead of a 403.

Move the map filter overlay to the bottom-right corner and color
decode contact paths by their decoded band so they match the band
legend and locator overlays.

Verified with cargo test -p trx-frontend-http.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

docs(spec): document ssh worker extension updates

Summary:
- SPEC의 SSH 워커 확장 및 상태 규칙 변경 내용 반영
- 후속 구현 작업 범위 고정을 위한 계획 문서 추가

Rationale:

Rationale:
- address PR feedback that approvals could silently stall during reconnects
- keep the auto-resume path from duplicating user messages in stored session history

feat(approvals): gate high-risk tool actions

Summary:
- add persisted approval requests plus API endpoints for listing, approving, and denying pending high-risk actions
- wrap high-risk tools with approval checkpoints and surface approval-required events in the chat flow
- add approval mode settings and a chat approval card so users can review and authorize risky actions

Rationale:
- continue the Season 1 trust-boundary work by turning high-risk tool use into an explicit user decision path
- build on the tool policy and audit log slices with a real checkpoint instead of only passive visibility

- 서버 바인딩, 포트 설정, 이벤트 처리, 스냅샷 추적 정보 보강
- 메모리 트래커 지원과 동적 폴링 간격 반영 추가

Rationale:

- 운영 중 문제 식별을 위한 세션 및 턴 단위 상태 정보 강화 필요

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(output): quiet mix test logging

Summary:
- capture logger output during ExUnit runs so passing tests stay quiet
- add a formatter that prints one-line unit and integration phase
  markers
- capture the expected simulator crash log in the crash-path test

Rationale:
- keep mix test output focused on progress and failures instead of
  runtime log noise
- preserve detailed logs for failed tests without hiding real failure
  context

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs:124
- mix test test/ethercat test/telemetry_test.exs
  test/integration/support/scenario_test.exs
- mix test (4 raw transport timeout failures remain in
  test/integration/simulator/00_healthy_ring_transport_matrix_test.exs)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(runtime): remove duplicated helper logic

Summary:
- extract shared simulator slave list and memory helpers to remove
  repeated update, fetch, bit, and binary replacement code
- consolidate common WKC/result checks in EtherCAT.Utils and simplify
  master, slave, and mailbox activation and recovery flows
- keep workflow-specific refactors local for activation, mailbox writes,
  and step runners while preserving existing behavior

Rationale:
- the repo had a cluster of exact clones across simulator, slave, and
  master runtime modules that added maintenance overhead
- narrow shared helpers keep the deduplication cohesive without adding
  another generic API utils layer
- local extractions remove repetition where reuse is intra-module only,
  which keeps boundaries explicit

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test --warnings-as-errors
- mix ex_dna

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(changelog): fill unreleased hash references

Summary:
- replace pending unreleased hash markers with the short hash for
  the release-surface hardening commit

Rationale:
- AGENTS.md requires release-note-worthy changelog entries to carry
  short hashes before handoff
- keep the unreleased section ready for release note extraction

Validation:
- not run (changelog-only update)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(repo): harden release surface and capture safety

Summary:
- move simulator reference notes out of the tracked lib tree and
  point simulator docs at local helper material
- make capture artifacts data-only .capture files and reject
  malformed payloads without evaluating code
- normalize public API and simulator call exits to return
  {:error, {:server_exit, reason}} and align release workflow and
  release metadata with raw-capable CI

Rationale:
- keep packaged artifacts reproducible and avoid shipping local
  helper material or executable capture files
- preserve caller safety when local servers die mid-call and ensure
  tag releases exercise the same transport surface as CI
- record the post-v0.3.1 work in the changelog before release prep

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test --warnings-as-errors
- mix format --check-formatted
- mix docs.fresh
- mix hex.build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore: Codex 로컬 워크플로우 스킬셋 추가

Summary:
- 로컬 브랜치 관리, 디버깅, 푸시, 머지용 Codex 스킬셋 추가
- 작업 디렉토리 초기화와 반복 절차의 문서 및 스크립트 정리

Rationale:
- 반복 운영 절차 표준화를 통한 에이전트 작업 품질 안정화 필요
- 사람과 에이전트가 공유하는 로컬 워크플로 기준 마련

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add man page generation coverage

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

Add completion command coverage

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

chore(repo): move LLM helper docs out of tree

Summary:
- remove the tracked docs tree from the repo surface and keep it
  as local-only helper material under an ignored path
- update repo-facing references so tracked files no longer point
  at the helper corpus
- move the README smart-cell image into a public asset path

Rationale:
- keep the repository focused on public source, tests, and module
  docs instead of LLM-only support material
- avoid baking local helper paths into tracked project guidance

Validation:
- not run (repo-structure and documentation-surface change only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add generated completion and metadata docs

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

feat(auth): enforce workspace deck authorization and stabilize e2e flows

Harden presentation access so users can only resolve and mutate decks in workspaces they belong to, preventing cross-workspace leakage.
Stabilize end-to-end coverage with deterministic URL-based fixtures and add focused authorization docs and tests so regressions are caught early.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Document pipeline stop terminal-state semantics

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

Libdl: In `Libdl.dlpath`, throw an ArgumentError if the handle is `C_NULL` (#61307)

🤖 Discovered by Codex during an audit.

Other functions in Libdl (such as `Libdl.dlsym`) already include a check
for `C_NULL`. For example:


https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/bc4bb7ae9a49808a929917d921e597906a9f30cb/base/libdl.jl#L59-L60

So this PR adds a `C_NULL` check to `Libdl.dlpath`, and (similar to
`Libdl.dlsym`), throws an `ArgumentError`.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: julia

commit.message:
Add upgrade mode and daily update check

* Add upgrade mode and daily update check

Implement a Codex-native upgrade audit mode, add a daily scheduled GitHub Actions check, and document the new maintenance workflow.

Validation:
- bun run typecheck
- bun run smoke

Closes: #5

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Add upgrade-check spec coverage

Add a dedicated upgrade-check spec and wire it into smoke so workflow changes land with real test coverage and the review gate stays meaningful.

Validation:
- bun run typecheck
- bun run smoke

Refs: #5

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs: AGENTS.md 작업 가이드 추가

Summary:
- 코딩 에이전트용 프로젝트 규칙 문서화
- 구조, 안전 규칙, 테스트 기준의 단일 문서 정리

Rationale:
- 자동화된 변경과 기존 설계 간 충돌 방지를 위한 제약 명시 필요
- 반복 작업 품질과 일관성 유지를 위한 공통 작업 규칙 필요

feat: WebにSentryを導入する

apps/web に Sentry の初期化処理とビルド設定を追加する。
デプロイ時の source map アップロード用設定と関連ドキュメントも更新する。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: Orchestrator::Persistable concern 추가

Summary:
- 오케스트레이터 이벤트와 영속 모델을 연결하는 퍼시스턴스 계층 추가
- 재시작 이후 실행 중 이슈와 재시도 상태 복원 지원

Rationale:
- 인메모리 상태만으로는 재기동 및 장애 이후 연속성 보장 불가
- 오케스트레이터 의사결정 근거의 데이터베이스 보존 필요

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Remove `@ts-nocheck` from the next two operational scripts and add explicit TypeScript types without changing their runtime behavior.

Validation:

- bun run smoke

Refs: #3

Harden pipeline stop integration behavior

Harden weight date validation

Add a calendar-validity helper for ISO dates so impossible values are rejected before the weight modal uses them, bump the app version to 1.4.58, sync the service worker cache to habits-v58, and update the README version line to match.
